posts.title: Census bureau projects that Trump's expanded immigration policies could leave the US with 100 million less people by the end of this century.

twitter.content: As President Trump expands immigration enforcement policies, Census Bureau projections show that sustained extremely low or zero net immigration could leave the U.S. population more than 107 million smaller by the end of the century, per NPR.

posts.raw_content:
I just thought it'd be nice to make one place on here  where we can talk about the movie.

My thoughts? all in all... not the greatest action film I've ever seen, it's no Die Hard, but it's pretty good, it moves at a decent pace, at under 90 minutes, definitely not too long, and obviously it's an important message to get out there, especially in regard to the migrant crime situation, especially in Europe.

Having said that, he's not a totally sympathetic character, like he did seem to kill a bunch of police officers unnecessarily in the bunker scene, and then the fireball that killed more police officer later seemed totally unnecessary (as he knew he'd be long gone by that point) other than he knew it would kill even more innocent police officers.

But he has his own code, so I get it. At his point... he's not like Dirty Harry or someone like that, who *only* kills bad guys.  I mean, he kills a bunch of people, perhaps not all of them deserving to die.  The most extreme example is when he was just trying to make a point to the judge about rules in society, and he drove a completely innocent car off the road and killed whoever was in it, even though they did nothing wrong other than "not break the rules".

Still, I was kind of shocked that a movie made in 2026 would take on this topic as forcibly as it did.  It was not subtle, at all.  And it's kind of wild that the world's richest man is posting it on online and getting behind it. As is all of the attention it's getting.

Anyway, I just watched it and I felt like I needed to talk to someone about it and I don't know anyone else that watched it so I thought I'd come on here and get y'all's thoughts. 

So, what do you guys think?

posts.content:
I just thought it'd be nice to make one place on here where we can talk about the movie.

My thoughts? all in all... not the greatest action film I've ever seen, it's no Die Hard, but it's pretty good, it moves at a decent pace, at under 90 minutes, definitely not too long, and obviously it's an important message to get out there, especially in regard to the migrant crime situation, especially in Europe.

Having said that, he's not a totally sympathetic character, like he did seem to kill a bunch of police officers unnecessarily in the bunker scene, and then the fireball that killed more police officer later seemed totally unnecessary (as he knew he'd be long gone by that point) other than he knew it would kill even more innocent police officers.

But he has his own code, so I get it. At his point... he's not like Dirty Harry or someone like that, who only kills bad guys. I mean, he kills a bunch of people, perhaps not all of them deserving to die. The most extreme example is when he was just trying to make a point to the judge about rules in society, and he drove a completely innocent car off the road and killed whoever was in it, even though they did nothing wrong other than "not break the rules".

Still, I was kind of shocked that a movie made in 2026 would take on this topic as forcibly as it did. It was not subtle, at all. And it's kind of wild that the world's richest man is posting it on online and getting behind it. As is all of the attention it's getting.
